name: Gauge hierarchy problem
summary: The Higgs mass m_H ~ 125 GeV is enormously smaller than the natural cutoff scale at which new physics is expected (Planck, GUT, or seesaw scales). Scalar masses receive quadratically divergent radiative corrections, so without a protective mechanism m_H^2 is fine-tuned to one part in (M_cutoff/m_H)^2.

characteristic_numbers.note: Squared in the mass-parameter sensitivity: tuning is ~10^34 against M_Planck cutoff, ~10^28 against GUT scale.

principles_stressed.note: Quadratic sensitivity of scalar masses to UV cutoff is the canonical motivation for naturalness as a guiding principle. LHC's null results for natural-scale new physics have made this puzzle sharper, not softer.

history: Articulated in the late 1970s in the context of grand unification (Gildener 1976, Weinberg 1979). Drove SUSY's appeal in the 1980s. Post-LHC, the absence of natural-scale BSM has prompted reformulations: relaxion (Graham-Kaplan-Rajendran 2015), 'split SUSY', anthropic / multiverse selection, or a re-examination of whether quadratic sensitivity is a real puzzle in dimensional regularisation.

references.citation: Gildener, 'Gauge symmetry hierarchies', Phys. Rev. D 14, 1667 (1976)
Weinberg, 'Implications of dynamical symmetry breaking', Phys. Rev. D 19, 1277 (1979)
Giudice, 'Naturally Speaking: The Naturalness Criterion and Physics at the LHC' (2008)
